name: /profile
description: Get your Control Board user profile
long_description:
Step 1: Call GET /api/user/me with Bearer token
Step 2: Format and display name, email, roles, and access level
Step 3: Show account status and last login info

name: /update-profile
long_description:
Step 1: Ask user which fields to update (name, company, phone)
Step 2: Validate provided fields
Step 3: PATCH /api/user/profile with updated fields
Step 4: Confirm update and show new profile

name: /settings
long_description:
Step 1: Read CONTROLBOARD_API_TOKEN from .env.personal
Step 2: GET /api/settings with Bearer token and X-Workspace-Id header
Step 3: Display all settings as key/value pairs

name: /save-setting
/control
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/control with Bearer token
Step 2: Format results showing apiKeys, models, cronJobs, tasks, channels, integrations, features
Step 3: Display to user with counts per section

name: /sync-control-full
description: Full sync of bot config to dashboard (replaces all sections)
long_description:
Step 1: Read ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json for current config
Step 2: Extract apiKeys, models, channels; use cron list tool for cronJobs
Step 3: POST /api/control with full payload (api_key_raw field for keys)
Step 4: Confirm sync — X API keys, Y models, Z channels now visible

name: /update-control
description: Partial update of Control Center (one section only)
long_description:
Step 1: Ask user which section to update (apiKeys/models/cronJobs/tasks/channels/integrations/features)
Step 2: Validate the JSON data for that section
Step 3: PATCH /api/control with only the changed section
Step 4: Confirm update successful

name: /sync-spend
description: Sync API spend from provider billing APIs
long_description:
Step 1: POST /api/control/sync-spend with Bearer token
Step 2: Wait for response (fetches from openai/anthropic/mistral billing APIs; groq is skipped)
Step 3: Report updated spend amounts per provider

name: /workspaces
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/workspaces with Bearer token
Step 2: Format results showing workspace id, name, slug
Step 3: Display list with count — note the active workspace ID needed for X-Workspace-Id header

name: /create-workspace
long_description:
Step 1: Ask user for workspace name
Step 2: POST /api/workspaces with { name }
Step 3: Note: first workspace creation migrates all existing data
Step 4: Confirm creation and show new workspace id — use this as X-Workspace-Id

name: /rename-workspace
/delete-workspace
description: Delete a workspace (cannot delete last one)
long_description:
Step 1: Fetch and display workspace details to user
Step 2: Warn: this deletes all workspace-scoped data; cannot delete last workspace
Step 3: Ask for explicit yes confirmation
Step 4: DELETE /api/workspaces with { id }
Step 5: Confirm deletion

name: /workspace-keys
description: View which API keys are configured per workspace
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/workspace-keys with Bearer token
Step 2: Format boolean flags per workspace (vizard_api_key, letterman_api_key, etc.)
Step 3: Display which keys are connected vs missing per workspace

name: /clips
description: List video clips with filtering and sorting
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/clips with X-Workspace-Id header; optional params: filter, category, sortBy
Step 2: Format results showing clip_id, title, status, viral_score, category
Step 3: Display stats (total, pending, approved, published, rejected) and categories

name: /clips-approved
description: Get all approved clips ready for publishing
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/clips/approved with X-Workspace-Id header
Step 2: Format results showing clip_id, title, viral_score, clip_url
Step 3: Display count and suggest /publish workflow via PostStream

name: /poll-clips
description: Poll Vizard API for new clips
long_description:
Step 1: Verify vizard_api_key and vizard_project_id are saved in settings
Step 2: POST /api/clips/poll with Bearer token and X-Workspace-Id header
Step 3: Report how many new clips were added and total count

name: /approve-clip
/reject-clip
/update-clip-status
/delete-clip
long_description:
Step 1: Fetch and display clip details to user
Step 2: Ask for explicit yes confirmation before proceeding
Step 3: DELETE /api/clips with X-Workspace-Id header: { clip_id } — note: uses clip_id field not id
Step 4: Confirm deletion successful

name: /videos
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/videos with X-Workspace-Id header; optional params: filter, category, sortBy
Step 2: Format results showing id, name, url, source, status, category
Step 3: Display stats and category counts

name: /add-video
description: Add a new video to the workspace
long_description:
Step 1: Ask for video name and URL (required); optionally source, category, category_emoji
Step 2: POST /api/videos with X-Workspace-Id header and video data
Step 3: Confirm video added with id — default status is pending_review

name: /update-video
description: Update a video's status or category
long_description:
Step 1: Ask for video id and fields to update (status, category, category_emoji)
Step 2: Valid statuses: pending_review | approved | published | rejected
Step 3: PUT /api/videos with X-Workspace-Id header: { id, ...fields }
Step 4: Confirm update successful

name: /delete-video
long_description:
Step 1: Fetch and display video details to user
Step 2: Ask for explicit yes confirmation before proceeding
Step 3: DELETE /api/videos with X-Workspace-Id header: { id }
Step 4: Confirm deletion successful

name: /video-categories
description: List all video categories for workspace
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/video-categories with X-Workspace-Id header
Step 2: Format results showing id, name, emoji
Step 3: Display list with count

name: /create-video-category
long_description:
Step 1: Ask for category name and optional emoji
Step 2: POST /api/video-categories with X-Workspace-Id header: { name, emoji }
Step 3: Note: duplicate names return 409 error
Step 4: Confirm category created

name: /delete-video-category
long_description:
Step 1: Fetch and display category details to user
Step 2: Ask for explicit yes confirmation
Step 3: DELETE /api/video-categories with X-Workspace-Id header: { id }
Step 4: Confirm deletion

name: /articles
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/articles with X-Workspace-Id header
Step 2: Format results showing id, title, status, publication, created_at
Step 3: Display to user with total count

name: /articles-approved
description: List approved articles ready for publishing
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/articles/approved with X-Workspace-Id header; optional: sort (newest_first|oldest_first|title), search
Step 2: Format results showing id, title, publication
Step 3: Display count and suggest publishing oldest first

name: /add-article
long_description:
Step 1: Ask for title, url, imageUrl (required); optionally description, storageId, publicationName
Step 2: POST /api/articles/add with X-Workspace-Id header
Step 3: Confirm article saved as draft

name: /create-article
description: Create article via Letterman with image upload
long_description:
Step 1: Ask for publicationId, publicationName, url (required); optionally keywords, imageBase64
Step 2: POST /api/articles/create with X-Workspace-Id header (requires letterman_api_key in settings)
Step 3: Confirm article created and show returned image_url

name: /approve-article
/reject-article
/draft-article
/publish-article
description: Mark article as published (must be in approve status)

name: /delete-article
long_description:
Step 1: Fetch and display article details to user
Step 2: Ask for explicit yes confirmation
Step 3: POST /api/articles/delete with X-Workspace-Id header: { id } — note: this is a POST not DELETE
Step 4: Confirm deletion

name: /update-article-status
/letterman-publications
long_description:
Step 1: Verify letterman_api_key is saved in settings
Step 2: GET /api/articles/publications with X-Workspace-Id header
Step 3: Display publications list with id and name — use id as storageId when adding articles

name: /social-posts
description: List social media posts with status filter
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/social-posts with X-Workspace-Id header; optional status filter: all|pending|approved|scheduled|published|rejected
Step 2: Format results showing id, title, platforms, format, status
Step 3: Display list with count

name: /create-social-post
description: Create a new social media post
long_description:
Step 1: Ask for title (required); optionally description, image_url, caption, platforms, format
Step 2: Valid platforms: instagram|youtube|tiktok|x|bluesky|linkedin; formats: image|video|text|carousel
Step 3: POST /api/social-posts with X-Workspace-Id header
Step 4: Confirm post created with pending status

name: /update-social-post
description: Approve, reject, or publish a social post

name: /delete-social-post
long_description:
Step 1: Fetch and display post details to user
Step 2: Ask for explicit yes confirmation
Step 3: DELETE /api/social-posts with X-Workspace-Id header: { id }
Step 4: Confirm deletion

name: /publications
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/publications with Bearer token
Step 2: Format results showing id, name, status, city, state
Step 3: Display list with count

name: /create-publication
long_description:
Step 1: Ask for name (required); optionally description, city, state, letterman_publication_id, letterman_url
Step 2: POST /api/publications with provided fields
Step 3: Confirm publication created with active status

name: /toggle-publication
/delete-publication
long_description:
Step 1: Fetch and display publication details
Step 2: Ask for explicit yes confirmation
Step 3: DELETE /api/publications with { id }
Step 4: Confirm deletion

name: /ideas
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/ideas with Bearer token
Step 2: Format results showing id, title, status, priority, category
Step 3: Display list with count

name: /create-idea
long_description:
Step 1: Ask for title (required); optionally description, category, status (active|archived), priority (low|medium|high), tags
Step 2: POST /api/ideas with provided fields
Step 3: Confirm idea created with id

name: /update-idea
/delete-idea
long_description:
Step 1: Fetch and display idea details
Step 2: Ask for explicit yes confirmation
Step 3: DELETE /api/ideas with { id }
Step 4: Confirm deletion

name: /bookmarks
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/bookmarks with Bearer token
Step 2: Format results showing id, title, url, category, status
Step 3: Display list with count

name: /create-bookmark
long_description:
Step 1: Ask for title (required); optionally url, description, category, status (read-later|read|archived), tags
Step 2: POST /api/bookmarks with provided fields
Step 3: Confirm bookmark created

name: /update-bookmark
/delete-bookmark
long_description:
Step 1: Fetch and display bookmark details
Step 2: Ask for explicit yes confirmation
Step 3: DELETE /api/bookmarks with { id }
Step 4: Confirm deletion

name: /businesses
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/businesses with Bearer token
Step 2: Format results showing id, name, columns, card count
Step 3: Display list with count

name: /add-business
/update-business
description: Update a business board (full document replace)
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/businesses to fetch current business state
Step 2: Apply changes to the business object (name, columns, cards, resources)
Step 3: POST /api/businesses with { action: 'update', business: { id, name, columns, cards, resources } } — this is a FULL replace
Step 4: Confirm update successful

name: /delete-business
long_description:
Step 1: Fetch and display business details
Step 2: Ask for explicit yes confirmation — this deletes all cards and resources
Step 3: DELETE /api/businesses with { id }
Step 4: Confirm deletion

name: /assets
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/assets with Bearer token
Step 2: Format results showing id, title, category, status, tags
Step 3: Display list with count

name: /create-asset
long_description:
Step 1: Ask for title (required); optionally description, category, status (active|planning|completed), tags
Step 2: POST /api/assets with provided fields
Step 3: Confirm asset created with id

name: /update-asset
/delete-asset
long_description:
Step 1: Fetch and display asset details
Step 2: Ask for explicit yes confirmation
Step 3: DELETE /api/assets with { id }
Step 4: Confirm deletion

name: /memory-banks
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/memory-banks with Bearer token
Step 2: Format results showing id, name, description
Step 3: Display list with count

name: /create-memory-bank
/delete-memory-bank
long_description:
Step 1: Fetch and display memory bank details
Step 2: Ask for explicit yes confirmation — this deletes all content inside
Step 3: DELETE /api/memory-banks with { id }
Step 4: Confirm deletion

name: /team-projects
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/team/projects with Bearer token
Step 2: Format results showing id, name, color, task_count
Step 3: Display list — must create a project before adding tasks

name: /create-team-project
/update-team-project
description: Update a team project name or color

name: /delete-team-project
long_description:
Step 1: Fetch and display project details including task count
Step 2: Ask for explicit yes confirmation
Step 3: DELETE /api/team/projects with { id }
Step 4: Confirm deletion

name: /team-tasks
description: List team tasks with optional project filter
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/team/tasks with Bearer token; optional ?project_id= to filter
Step 2: Format results showing id, title, status, priority, assignee, due_date, progress
Step 3: Display list with count

name: /create-team-task
long_description:
Step 1: Ask for title (required); optionally description, status (inbox|assigned|in_progress|review|done), priority (low|medium|high), project_id, assignee, due_date, tags, progress (0-100)
Step 2: POST /api/team/tasks with provided fields
Step 3: Confirm task created

name: /update-team-task
/delete-team-task
long_description:
Step 1: Fetch and display task details
Step 2: Ask for explicit yes confirmation
Step 3: DELETE /api/team/tasks with { id }
Step 4: Confirm deletion

name: /commands
description: List all command shortcuts in registry
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/commands with Bearer token
Step 2: Format results showing id, name, description, category
Step 3: Display list with count

name: /create-command
long_description:
Step 1: Ask for name and description (required); optionally long_description (newline-separated steps), category (Resources|External Software|Titanium), tags
Step 2: POST /api/commands with provided fields
Step 3: Confirm command created

name: /update-command
/delete-command
long_description:
Step 1: Fetch and display command details
Step 2: Ask for explicit yes confirmation
Step 3: DELETE /api/commands?id=<commandId> — note: uses query param not body
Step 4: Confirm deletion

name: /setup-commands
description: Install all default Control Board commands to dashboard
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/commands to check which commands already exist
Step 2: Load commands.json from skill directory
Step 3: POST each missing command with long_description, category, tags
Step 4: Report: X commands installed, Y already existed

name: /sync-all-commands
description: Scan all installed skills and sync their commands
long_description:
Step 1: List all skill directories under ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/
Step 2: For each skill read commands.json or parse SKILL.md for slash commands
Step 3: GET /api/commands to check existing; POST only new commands
Step 4: Report total synced across all skills

name: /sync-skill-commands
description: Sync commands from a specific skill
long_description:
Step 1: Ask for skill name or directory path
Step 2: Read that skill's commands.json or parse its SKILL.md
Step 3: GET /api/commands to check existing; POST only new commands
Step 4: Confirm how many commands synced for that skill

name: /vault
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/vault/items with Bearer token
Step 2: Format results showing id, title, type, category, url
Step 3: Display list with count

name: /create-vault-item
description: Store a new item in the vault
long_description:
Step 1: Ask for title (required); optionally category, type (Image|File|Screenshot|PPT|URL|Video|Text|Design Link), url, notes, tags
Step 2: POST /api/vault/items with provided fields
Step 3: Confirm vault item created with id

name: /shopping
long_description:
Step 1: GET /api/shopping with Bearer token
Step 2: Format results showing id, title, price, status, url
Step 3: Display list with count

name: /add-shopping-item
description: Add a new item to the wish list
long_description:
Step 1: Ask for title (required); optionally url, price, image, description, category, status (to-buy|watching|archived), tags
Step 2: POST /api/shopping with provided fields
Step 3: Confirm item added

name: /update-shopping-item
